title: Amazon S3 US-EAST-1 Service Disruption
summary: A maintenance command used during S3 billing troubleshooting removed more index and placement capacity than intended, causing a regional S3 outage and downstream service impact.
impactDetails: GET/LIST/PUT/DELETE API impact in us-east-1
Dependent services including EC2 launch, EBS from snapshots, and Lambda affected
Prolonged recovery due to full restart and integrity checks on large subsystems
learnings: Add hard capacity floor guardrails to operational tooling
Partition critical subsystems to reduce restart blast radius
Design status tooling to avoid dependency on affected service

title: Meta Global Backbone Outage
summary: A backbone capacity command unintentionally disconnected data centers globally, withdrawing DNS-related BGP advertisements and making Meta services unreachable.
impactDetails: Global unavailability of Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and internal tooling
Loss of DNS reachability due to BGP withdrawal behavior
Recovery required physical access and controlled service ramp-up
learnings: Strengthen command audit and preflight validation in backbone workflows
Exercise recovery under full DNS and internal tool loss scenarios
Control service reintroduction to avoid secondary traffic surges

title: Fastly Global Outage from Valid Configuration Trigger
summary: A latent software bug in an earlier deployment was triggered by a valid customer configuration, resulting in widespread global errors.
impactDetails: Large portions of edge network returned errors
Major customer websites and APIs became unavailable
Rapid identification and isolation restored most traffic quickly
learnings: Stress trigger-path testing for valid but rare configuration combinations
Isolate triggering configurations quickly and safely
Deploy permanent fix after immediate mitigation

title: GitLab Database Outage and Data Loss Event
summary: During ongoing database stress, an operator command was executed on the primary instead of the secondary, deleting substantial production data and forcing complex restoration.
impactDetails: Extended platform outage
Data loss window despite partial recovery
Backup and recovery process gaps surfaced under stress
learnings: Harden primary/secondary safety checks on destructive commands
Continuously validate backup restorability in realistic drills
Document and rehearse high-pressure incident runbooks

title: Atlassian Site Deletion Incident
summary: A script intended to remove a legacy app was run with site IDs instead of app IDs, deleting hundreds of customer cloud sites and requiring multi-day restoration.
impactDetails: 775 customers affected
Restoration lasted up to 14 days for some customers
Large-scale coordinated data and identifier reconstruction
learnings: Enforce typed identifier validation in destructive APIs and scripts
Build restoration automation for large-scale multi-service recovery
Preserve critical identifiers for easier restoration mapping

title: CrowdStrike Falcon Windows Sensor Content Incident
summary: A Rapid Response Content configuration update passed validation with problematic data and caused Windows host crashes on impacted systems.
impactDetails: Large-scale Windows endpoint instability
Emergency customer remediation workflows needed
Reverted channel content and host-by-host recovery
learnings: Treat dynamic content validation as production-critical control plane
Strengthen rollout gates and independent quality checks
Prepare offline and automated recovery pathways for endpoints

title: Google Cloud Filestore Global API Throttling Incident
summary: An internal service malfunction overloaded Filestore APIs, triggering global throttling under a global quota design and breaking read-only API calls.
impactDetails: Read-only API access failures across locations
Console and CLI list/get operations affected
Mutating operations largely continued but observability degraded
learnings: Avoid global choke points where regional isolation is expected
Pause offending internal traffic sources quickly
Improve quota saturation early warning and mitigation automation

title: Google Cloud Service Control Crash Loop Incident
summary: A policy change with unintended blank fields exercised an unprotected code path in Service Control, causing global crash loops and broad API impact.
impactDetails: Widespread 503/API failures across Google Cloud products
Global propagation from replicated policy metadata
Regional recovery variance with prolonged us-central1 effects
learnings: Feature-flag and error-handle all new control-plane paths
Use fast kill-switches for high-blast-radius policy logic
Plan recovery controls for thundering-herd restart behavior

title: Cloudflare Service Token Metadata Incident
summary: A release for service-token last-seen metadata overwrote token secrets for impacted internal accounts, causing authentication failures across multiple Cloudflare services.
impactDetails: WARP and Zero Trust enrollment/authentication impact
Control-plane degradation for API-related products
Staggered impact due to token sync/update behavior
learnings: Never write back redacted reads without full-field integrity checks
Protect critical token fields with stronger constraints
Use layered restoration paths: immediate manual plus database recovery

title: Cloudflare 1.1.1.1 DNSSEC Lookup Failures
summary: Resolver systems served from a stale root zone after parser failures on a newer root record type, leading to elevated SERVFAIL responses when signatures expired.
impactDetails: Global but uneven SERVFAIL increase
Peak error rates concentrated in large POPs
Initial override attempt only partially effective before full stop of preloading
learnings: Monitor stale-root serving directly as a first-class SLO signal
Handle unknown record types and parser failures with safer fallback behavior
Validate mitigation paths under forwarded-query behavior
